title: Audit and consolidate Bureau scheduler lanes
goal: Map every active Bureau timer to its inputs, outputs, ordering constraints, unique consumer and failure behavior, then consolidate only lanes whose semantics are actually redundant.

acceptance.assertion: All active Bureau timers are inventoried from live systemd state with unit source, cadence and last result.
Each lane names its unique output and actual consumer, or is marked unconsumed.
Overlapping lanes are grouped into an explicit staged scheduler plan before any disablement.
Any timer disablement has rollback, bounded runtime proof and no loss of Bureau Core authority.

validation: All active Bureau user timers were read from live systemd state with source, cadence, last trigger and service result.
Inputs, outputs, ordering and known consumers were mapped from live units, journals, state artifacts and repository source.
No timer, service, state file or runtime configuration was changed.
Codex bridge was classified as condition-skipped and drifted rather than successful.
Verifier systemd success was kept distinct from application partial/degraded health.
verification.state_transition_semantics: Bureau task schema has no active or resolved task state. The audit moved from planned to verified after live evidence, artifact validation and explicit follow-up registration; no runtime effect was claimed.

hash_semantics.task_sha256: Canonical Bureau task revision hash excluding mutable state and verification metadata.
hash_semantics.plan_sha256: Canonical hash of the initiative current_plan object required by Bureau verification validation.
hash_semantics.report_sha256: Byte hash of report_path, the machine-readable live scheduler inventory.
hash_semantics.audit_plan_sha256: Byte hash of plan_path, the scheduler consolidation plan produced by this audit.

validated_acceptance.timer-inventory: The machine report inventories all 10 active user timers and records no system-wide Bureau timers, including unit source, cadence and last result.
validated_acceptance.consumer-map: Each lane records inputs, outputs and known consumers; GPT probe and Codex bridge are explicitly marked as lacking a proven current consumer.
validated_acceptance.consolidation-plan: The plan retains eight distinct lanes and stages Codex/GPT/deployment-source decisions separately before any runtime effect.
validated_acceptance.safe-cutover: No disablement occurred. The plan requires exact live preflight, rollback capture and bounded post-effect proof for every future cutover.
